title: Set Google calendar owner as monday item assignee

posts.author: RyanBY (Ryan BY)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
So I’m trying to sync “My Google calendar” (where “My” is dynamic and works according to the logged in user) into Monday and want all synced events to be assigned to the logged in user.

Any idea on how to do it please?
Thanks.

posts.author: Dani (Dani Lester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @RyanBY

We do offer a two way Google Calendar Integration, which allows you to automatically sync your items from monday.com to your Google Calendar, and vice versa.
Currently only user can connect to one calendar, so each user would need to set this up individually for their own personal Google Calendar with the relevant monday.com user profile.

You can read more on this here:
Google Calendar Integration

Would this be an option for you here?

Best,

Dani

posts.author: RyanBY (Ryan BY)
posts.cookedHtml:
Cheers Dani,
Saying both user X and user Y set the integration,
I want each user to “own” his/ her events, hence I was thinking to set the event’s creator (taken from Google calendar) as monday pulse assignee, and yet it doesn’t work.

Am I missing anything?

posts.author: mfriedma (Michael Friedman)
posts.cookedHtml: I also need an answer to this question.

posts.author: mfriedma (Michael Friedman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dani, you do not seem to have understood the issue.

When the Google Calendar item is imported into Monday it needs to be assigned to the user it was imported from. So items imported from my calendar get assigned to me. How do we make this happen?
